institution_name: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
framework_alignment: Virginia Executive Order 41 (2011); VDEM/Commonwealth COOP; all-hazards; essential-functions based
evidence_quote: A departmental Continuity of Operations Plans (COOP) is designed to provide guidance for sustaining departmental essential functions in response to an emergency incident, so that departments can effectively maintain operations through whatever challenges may come. The departmental COOP includes procedures for addressing three types of disruptions to operations that could result in a loss of services: Loss of a facility, or portion of a facility; Loss of personnel / reduction in workforce; and Equipment or utilities failure.
catalog_notes: University-published COOP Development Guide/template for departments; managed in Veoci Emergency Planning Portal. COOP Narrative + Essential Functions sections. Annual update by March 15 required by VT policies 5615/1005 and Gov. EO 41 (2011). Departmental plans themselves are restricted; this is the public university-published template/guide.

framework_alignment: Virginia Executive Order 41; VDEM Continuity Plan Template; all-hazards; UVA Policy SEC-001
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations Plans (COOP) A functional plan providing procedures to continue operations in the wake of an incident where the normal operations of the University, school, or department are severely disrupted.
catalog_notes: UVA Office of Emergency Management publishes redacted public COOP PDFs annually (2022 public, 2024/2025/2026 redacted) on its official host. Quote verified verbatim from the rendered UVA Policy glossary page tied to Policy SEC-001 (Emergency Management and Continuity of Operations). The dated COOP PDFs are real files on uvaemergency.virginia.edu but did not render as text in this session; verification rests on the rendered official policy page.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; critical-functions / 30-day continuity period; ECU Ready platform
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations (COOP) Plans are intended to guide departments, divisions, and the University as a whole in the continued provisions of essential operations and the re-establishment of critical business functions during and after a disaster occurs. ... A departmental COOP plan may be comprised of the following components: Identification of Critical Functions; Succession of Leadership; Delegation of Authorities; Communication Plan; Vital Data, Systems and Equipment; Upstream and Downstream Dependencies; Key Resources.
catalog_notes: ECU's COOP program (Office of Environmental Health and Safety / Emergency Management). Departmental COOP template integrated into 'ECU Ready' platform. Office of Internal Audit audits continuity plans annually. Companion 'Continuity of Instruction' site exists separately.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; primary business functions / mission essential functions; 30-day continuity period; VDEM/EO 41
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations Plan This plan provides a framework to minimize potential impact and allow for rapid recovery from an incident that disrupts operations. This plan encompasses the magnitude of operations and services performed by the university, and is tailored to the university's unique operations, primary business functions, and mission essential functions. This plan identifies key personnel that perform or manage primary business functions (PBF). This plan was prepared by George Mason University to develop, implement and maintain a viable continuity capability.
catalog_notes: GMU Risk, Safety & Resilience administers the COOP via a remote-hosted continuity planning software (separate formats for administrative units and academic colleges). University Policy requires all units to create a COOP covering critical functions for 30 calendar days. Separate Research Continuity Guidelines also exist.

institution_name: University of North Carolina at Greensboro
framework_alignment: All-hazards; mission-critical functions; UNC Policy Manual 300.8.3[R]; Veoci platform
evidence_quote: A Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) documents how your department or business unit will perform essential operations during an emergency situation or long-term disruption, which might last from two days to several weeks. The plan identifies mission-critical functions, departmental communication methods, and alternate personnel, systems and locations. Each University department needs a COOP to ensure the University can respond effectively to a variety of situations.
catalog_notes: UNCG Office of Emergency Management; continuity plans built/maintained in Veoci. Chancellor requires each business unit providing essential functions to develop and maintain a COOP (University Policy Manual, approved 2018-08-13). Covers orders of succession, delegation of authority, vital records, upstream/downstream dependencies, annual review/testing.

framework_alignment: UNCW EOP requirement; UNC Policy Manual 300.8.3[R]; all-hazards critical-functions
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) - A written document that details a division's, department's, or program's (collectively, "units") capability to continue essential program functions and services ... Unit leaders, within all divisions, are required to develop and maintain a COOP that addresses the critical functions and mission of that unit. ... UNCW maintains a COOP Template to provide direction for the process of mitigation, preparation response, and recovery from emergencies and disasters for that particular unit.
catalog_notes: UNCW Policy 05.610 (Environmental Health & Safety/Emergency Management). University-maintained COOP template; annual review commencing April 1 by COOP manager. Implements the EOP requirement that departments plan for continuity of critical operations.

framework_alignment: VDEM Continuity of Operations Planning Manual; Commonwealth of Virginia; BIA + RTO; IT DR
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plan (BCP/COOP) is Old Dominion's documented plan and set of procedures created to assure that the capability exists to continue essential University functions across a wide range of potential emergencies. ... Based on Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and Risk Assessment (RA) results, ODU will develop COOP IT-related documentation which identifies: Essential business functions that require restoration and the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for each; Recovery requirements for IT systems and data needed to support the essential business functions; and Personnel contact information and incident notification procedures.
catalog_notes: ODU IT Standard 07.2.0 - Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery Plan Standard. Defines IT-related COOP requirements, designates the Information Security Officer as COOP/DR liaison, requires annual DR testing and 12-month DRP review; aligns IT requirements to the VDEM Continuity of Operations Planning Manual. ODU also maintains a separate University Continuity (BCP/COOP) plan.

framework_alignment: NC GS 147-33.89; State of NC ITS 18.05 Business Continuity Management Policy; business/mission/academic/research continuity + IT DR
evidence_quote: This regulation addresses the responsibilities of university divisions, colleges, departments and units to identify critical services and processes and to develop, maintain, and exercise academic, business, mission and research continuity and IT disaster recovery plans in coordination with the Department of Emergency Management and Mission Continuity ... Resiliency and continuity plans consist of four key elements: business, mission, academic and research continuity. Resiliency and continuity plan development is predicated on risk assessment and prioritization of continuity requirements.
catalog_notes: NC State REG 04.00.07 'Developing University Continuity and IT Disaster Recovery Plans' (Pack Ready plans). Establishes Emergency Planning & Mission Continuity Steering Committee, a Research Continuity & Recovery Committee, and Academic Continuity Plans (ACPs). Five-year university-wide Risk Assessment and IT BIA; annual testing of continuity plans for critical services.

framework_alignment: All-hazards (natural, manmade, technological, WMD); 30-day essential functions; VDEM/EO 41
evidence_quote: The all-hazards approach to COOP planning ensures UMW faculty, staff, students and visitors that regardless of the emergency, essential functions and services will continue to operate and be provided in some capacity. ... It also provides policy and guidance to ensure the capability to implement actions to continue essential functions within the recovery priority timeframes established by the UMW COOP Team to maintain essential functions for up to thirty (30) days. ... COOP is the preservation, maintenance, and reconstitution of a universities ability to deliver its essential functions to faculty, staff, students and visitors.
catalog_notes: UMW Emergency Management & Safety. Basic COOP Plan plus departmental COOPs across 12 named departments/divisions (e.g., Public Safety, HR, Registrar, Business & Finance, IT, Office of the President). Plans distributed via department heads and a secure website; COOP coordinators trained on activation.

framework_alignment: VDEM Continuity Plan Template; Commonwealth of Virginia Executive Order #41 (2019); mission essential functions
evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) provides the framework for how Radford University will continue operations and perform mission essential functions (MEFs) and associated business functions in the event of an incident, emergency or other event that disrupts normal operations. ... The COOP is not an emergency response plan. It is a recovery plan that works as a companion plan with the Crisis and Emergency Management Plan and the IT Disaster Recovery Plan. ... This plan was developed through a formal planning process involving institutional stakeholders and is compliant with the Virginia Department of Emergency Management (VDEM) Continuity Plan Template as required in Executive Order #41 (2019).
catalog_notes: Radford University Office of Emergency Management. COOP addresses loss of facility, loss of utilities/equipment, and reduced workforce; companion to the CEMP and IT Disaster Recovery Plan; explicitly VDEM/EO 41 (2019) compliant.

institution_name: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
framework_alignment: All-hazards; lifelines framework; 30-day recovery; UNC-Chapel Hill Policy on Emergency Management; Kuali continuity software
evidence_quote: Mission Continuity is the University's comprehensive Continuity of Operations (COOP) program, designed to ensure that mission-critical functions can be sustained or quickly restored during and after any disruptive event. ... Through Mission Continuity planning, departments and critical services across campus identify key operations, outline essential resources, and develop strategies to maintain or resume operations within 30 days of an interruption. The program focuses on three primary areas of recovery: Continuity of personnel. Work area recovery. Restoration of IT and utility services.
catalog_notes: UNC-Chapel Hill 'Mission Continuity' is the institution's COOP program, run by Emergency Management and Planning using Kuali continuity software. Lifeline subcomponents required to maintain plans. Designates Department Head, Continuity Plan Manager, and IT Plan Manager roles; BIA-style impact analysis and IT resilience built into Kuali.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
framework_alignment: Academic continuity / continuity of instruction; remote-delivery contingency (Canvas, Zoom, YuJa)
evidence_quote: University of Kentucky Continuity of Instruction Guidelines for Teaching During Disruptions or Closure This document is presented as a starting place for colleges and other academic units to determine plans for continuing the instruction of courses if there were temporary disruptions to the academic schedule or a campus closure.
catalog_notes: UK 'Continuity of Instruction' guidelines (Teaching, Learning, and Academic Innovation, Provost area). College-wide faculty survey, deans/chairs checklist, remote-delivery scenarios (short-term closure, semester closure), synchronous/asynchronous instruction. Distinct academic-continuity document; UK also requires a separate Business Continuity Plan (BCP) program via UK Police Crisis Management & Preparedness.

framework_alignment: FEMA CPG 101; TEMA plan template; EMAP standards; T.C.A. 58-2-108; full FCD-style continuity elements
evidence_quote: Each campus and institute must develop and maintain a continuity plan to include essential functions, essential records, IT functions, human resources, communications, alternate locations and remote work, budget and acquisition, reconstitution, testing, training, and exercising and leadership succession to ensure capabilities can continue in the event of an emergency. ... Each campus and institute is responsible for designating a campus continuity manager to ensure continuity plans are completed in alignment with the system continuity plan template.
catalog_notes: UT System policy SA0200 (Emergency Management) sets system-wide continuity (COOP) requirements and a system continuity plan template binding all campuses/institutes (UTK, UTC, UTM, UTHSC, UT Southern, IPS, UTIA, UT Space Institute). Enumerates essential functions, essential records, reconstitution, leadership succession, TT&E, alternate locations -- strong FCD-element coverage. Review at least every 3 years; external review every 5 years.

framework_alignment: BIA + RTO/RPO; gap analysis; risk assessment; IT disaster recovery; annual testing
evidence_quote: Effective business continuity and disaster recovery plans are required in all areas of the University. Each school, unit and division must develop plans that will allow it to perform its core-required operations in an alternative fashion as well as an appropriate disaster recovery policy for their working environment. ... An effective Business Continuity Plan (BCP) contains the steps outlined below ... Perform Gap Analysis. Conduct Risk Assessment. Perform Business Impact Analysis. Determine Continuity/Recovery Strategy.
catalog_notes: UofL administrative policy ISO-002 'Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery' (Information Security Compliance Office). Defines BCP steps including gap analysis, risk assessment, BIA with RTO and RPO, off-site storage, annual training/testing and annual plan review. Required for all schools/units/divisions.

framework_alignment: VDEM Continuity Plan Template; Commonwealth of Virginia Executive Order #41 (2019); mission essential functions
evidence_quote: VCU utilizes continuity plans to ensure its operations are performed with minimal disruption during all-hazards emergencies or other situations that disrupt normal operations. This document provides planning and program guidance for implementing the COOP and Continuity Program to ensure the university is capable of conducting its mission essential functions (MEFs) under all threats and conditions. ... It is compliant with the Virginia Department of Emergency Management (VDEM) Continuity Plan Template and Commonwealth of Virginia Executive Order #41 (2019).
catalog_notes: VCU Division of Emergency Management. The COOP is described in detail on the official VCU Police Emergency Management 'University Plans' page (rendered), but the COOP document itself is withheld from the public due to sensitivity (contact EmergencyMGMT@vcu.edu). VDEM/EO 41 (2019) compliant; companion to the CEMP and Hazard Mitigation Plan.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; essential functions; relocation to alternate site; companion to Emergency Response Plan; VDEM/EO 41
evidence_quote: The purpose of the Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is the rapid recovery from the effects of a man-made, technological or natural disaster. The COOP covers essential university operations, schools, departments and service areas, including contracted operations. ... Using an all-hazards approach, the plan ensures that regardless of the event, essential functions will continue to operate and services will continue to be provided to the extent possible to faculty, staff and students ... the relocation of essential functions to an alternative site for an extended period of time.
catalog_notes: William & Mary COOP (Emergency Management, Public Safety). Companion to W&M's Emergency Response Plan; Emergency Management Team operates under the President. Verbatim quote captured from the official W&M COOP page; the page itself could not be re-rendered in this session due to fetch rate limiting, hence link-only.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; departmental COOP procedures; VDEM/EO 41; JMU Policy 1112
evidence_quote: JMU has adopted a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP), and a copy can be obtained from the JMU Emergency Management Coordinator. Each department and partner organization should develop and maintain departmental emergency plans and continuity of operations (COOP) procedures to effectively meet their responsibilities.
catalog_notes: JMU Policy 1112 'Emergency Response and Recovery' (Office of Risk Management / Office of Emergency Management) references the university COOP and requires departmental COOP procedures. A PDF of the policy is published at jmu.edu/jmu-policy/policies/pdfs/1112.pdf. Verbatim quote captured from the official JMU policy page; could not re-render bytes in this session due to fetch rate limiting, hence link-only.

institution_name: University of North Carolina at Charlotte
framework_alignment: BIA + risk assessment; critical business functions; University Resiliency Planner software; UNC System
evidence_quote: Each college, department and business unit is expected to complete and/or update a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) as part of a campus wide effort to assess critical business functions, interdependencies and address contingency planning challenges. A Business Impact Analysis (BIA) is conducted and a risk assessment is produced to inform business continuity planning decisions following completion of COOP plans. ... University Resiliency Planner is the system used for colleges, departments and business units to create Continuity Of Operations Plans (COOPs).
catalog_notes: UNC Charlotte Office of Emergency Management Business Continuity Planning program; units build COOPs in 'University Resiliency Planner'; BIA and risk assessment conducted; exercises validate plans. Verbatim quote captured from the official charlotte.edu page; could not re-render bytes in this session due to fetch rate limiting, hence link-only.

institution_name: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
framework_alignment: Academic continuity / continuity of instruction; Canvas LMS, Zoom, Kaltura; TLOS Continuity Partners
evidence_quote: All courses at Virginia Tech have an associated site in Canvas, the learning management system, which can help facilitate class continuity in the event of class cancellations by providing a secure, easily-accessible place for students to access materials and communicate.
catalog_notes: Virginia Tech 'Continuity of Instruction' resources from Technology-enhanced Learning and Online Strategies (TLOS); distinct academic-continuity genre separate from VT's departmental COOP program. Best practices for course materials, recorded/real-time lectures, online office hours; supported by TLOS Continuity Partners. Verbatim quote captured from the official tlos.vt.edu page; could not re-render bytes in this session due to fetch rate limiting, hence link-only.

framework_alignment: NIMS/ICS; Tennessee Emergency Management Plan; mission-essential functions; departmental/site-specific COOP
evidence_quote: Individual departments within the University shall have their departmental or site specific Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP). The COOP is a stand-alone document, which serves as an operational tool to ensure mission-essential functions and services are maintained.
catalog_notes: Tennessee Tech's Emergency Operations Plan (Environmental Health & Safety) describes departmental/site-specific COOPs as stand-alone operational tools for mission-essential functions; the President has authority to activate the COOP. The EOP PDF is published on the official tntech.edu host; verbatim COOP quote captured from the official source but the full PDF could not be re-rendered (too large / fetch rate limiting), hence link-only.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; required departmental BCPs; UK Police Crisis Management and Preparedness
evidence_quote: As you pursue academic and research excellence on campus, all UK departments and units are required to complete a BCP to ensure your department is prepared to respond to various types of operational interruptions. This BCP will help you plan not only for major disasters (e.g. total loss of a building) but also lesser interruptions to service (e.g., the computers are down).
catalog_notes: University of Kentucky requires all departments/units to complete a Business Continuity Plan (BCP). Administered by UK Police Department's Crisis Management and Preparedness division with a Business Continuity Coordinator. This is UK's operational continuity program (distinct from the UK academic Continuity of Instruction guidelines record).
